element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Powerful spear variant available via debug cheat
Obtainable via debug cheat alongside weapons
Escalating difficulty through stages 1-4 with story cutscenes
Related to Three-Eyed One civilization plot

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension through five stages with narrative relief via cutscenes after bosses 1-4; final stage lacks cutscene, proceeding directly to ending

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden level select accessible via cheat code; stage select implies non-linear or revisitable structure

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic platforming and early boss encounter (Stage 1)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating boss difficulty through Stages 2-3 with intermediate platforming challenges
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final stage progression without post-boss cutscene, direct ending sequence; increased difficulty implied
notable_spikes: Stage 4 boss (lead to unused/removed Stage 5 content)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of first four stages concludes with boss fight followed by story cutscene featuring defeated boss and Japanese text narrative. Cutscenes serve as pacing breaks and story advancement.

Stages structured around investigating mysterious ruins tied to Three-Eyed Ones civilization. Locations reference real-world archaeological sites (Easter Island implied by unused Stage 5 cutscene data).
Spear weapon and bird companion available as progression elements; debug cheat suggests these were designed as collectable power-ups or unlockables.
